channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: UAE announces exit from OPEC and OPEC+ amid Iran war energy crisis
item.description: The United Arab Emirates will leave OPEC on May 1, dealing a major blow to the oil cartel as the Iran-US conflict strains Gulf relations and reshapes global energy dynamics. The exit weakens OPEC+ influence and raises prospects of a price war.

item.title: Sexual violence in Sudan creates mental health crisis, UN warns
item.description: The WHO reports a mental health emergency in Sudan as sexual violence affects tens of thousands of women and girls amid the ongoing civil war. Access to healthcare is severely limited, with survivors facing stigma and lack of trained medical staff.

item.title: Jury deliberates in trial of alleged ISIS militant charged in Kabul airport bombing
item.description: Federal jurors in Virginia are weighing charges against Mohammad Sharifullah, accused of helping plan the 2021 Abbey Gate suicide bombing that killed 13 US service members and 160 Afghans during the chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan.

item.title: Canada reports lower deficit as oil price surge boosts revenues
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney unveiled a spring budget showing a deficit 14% lower than projected, aided by rising oil prices. The government announced a new sovereign wealth fund for infrastructure and domestic investments.

item.title: Oil prices surge as US moves to extend Iran port blockade
item.description: Oil prices extended their rally on reports the US will extend its blockade of Iranian ports, likely prolonging supply disruptions from the Middle East. The move signals no immediate end to the conflict that has constrained global oil flows.

item.title: Trump says Iran in collapse, wants US to reopen Hormuz Strait
item.description: President Trump stated that Iran has said it is in a state of collapse and wants the US to open the Strait of Hormuz while sorting out its leadership. The comment suggests Tehran is seeking an off-ramp from the prolonged conflict.

item.title: US imposes sanctions on 35 entities for Iran sanctions evasion
item.description: The US government ratcheted up pressure on Tehran, sanctioning 35 entities and individuals for roles in Iran shadow banking sector, and threatened sanctions against banks dealing with Chinese teapot refineries paying Hormuz tolls.

item.title: US bond markets split as Iran war tests Federal Reserve outlook
item.description: US bond markets show divergent views on the economic impact of the Iran conflict, with some investors seeking safety while others bet on inflationary pressures from oil disruptions. The Fed faces a critical decision on rates.

item.title: VLCC tanker attempts Hormuz crossing amid US-Iran tensions
item.description: Shipping data shows the Idemitsu Maru VLCC tanker is attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz, a rare move as most commercial vessels avoid the chokepoint due to the ongoing conflict and US blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: Former FBI Director Comey indicted for second time
item.description: The US Justice Department has indicted former FBI Director James Comey for a second time, CNN reports. The move comes amid ongoing political tensions between Trump administration officials and law enforcement.

item.title: King Charles to promote British-American unity in rare speech to US Congress
item.description: King Charles III will address a joint session of Congress in a rare speech aimed at strengthening British-American ties amid the global turmoil from the Iran conflict and shifting geopolitical alliances.

item.title: Trump approval rating sinks to record low as Iran war drives cost concerns
item.description: President Trump's approval rating has fallen to a record low as the war with Iran drives public concerns about rising costs of living, including fuel and consumer goods affected by supply chain disruptions.

item.title: Global military spending reaches record $4 trillion
item.description: World military expenditure hit a record $4 trillion as nations ramp up defense budgets in response to growing geopolitical tensions, including the Iran conflict and ongoing Ukraine-Russia war.

item.title: SK Group chairman suggests South Korea pursue economic integration with Japan
item.description: SK Group's chairman proposed that South Korea should pursue economic integration with Japan, highlighting growing cooperation between the two US allies amid regional security challenges.

item.title: Five ways Iran war reveals NATO weakness vs Russia conflict
item.description: The Iran conflict has exposed vulnerabilities in NATO's ability to respond to simultaneous threats, highlighting gaps in alliance coordination as the Ukraine-Russia war continues.

item.title: Hormuz shipping traffic remains muted with no US-Iran deal in sight
item.description: Shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz remains constrained as negotiations between the US and Iran show no sign of progress, keeping the vital waterway largely closed to commercial traffic.

item.title: Intercontinental ballistic missiles pose growing threat to United States
item.description: ICBM threats against the US homeland are increasing as major powers expand their strategic missile capabilities, prompting renewed focus on missile defense systems.

item.title: Chinese team pioneers path to turn carbon dioxide into jet fuel
item.description: Chinese researchers have developed a method to convert carbon dioxide into jet fuel, potentially revolutionizing aviation fuel production as energy prices soar globally.

item.title: Resistance to new mosques exposes tensions over Japan's growing Muslim communities
item.description: Japan faces social tensions as its Muslim population grows, with local opposition emerging to new mosque construction despite the country's need for foreign workers.

item.title: India faces economic shocks from Iran-Hormuz war
item.description: India's economy is suffering from the ongoing Iran conflict and Hormuz closure, with rising energy costs and trade disruptions deepening the country's economic wounds despite ceasefire talks.

item.title: Powell's legacy as Fed defender-in-chief
item.description: Jerome Powell's greatest legacy may be his role as the Federal Reserve's defender against political pressure, as he prepares to step down amid economic turbulence from the Iran conflict.

item.title: Crude oil may stay elevated despite UAE's OPEC exit
item.description: Analysts say crude oil prices are likely to remain high despite the UAE leaving OPEC, as the Strait of Hormuz closure continues to constrain global oil supplies from the region.

item.title: Israel-Iran war: Tehran labels US ship seizures 'piracy' in UN protest
item.description: Iran formally protested to the UN Secretary-General, calling US ship seizures in the region "piracy" as the conflict continues to disrupt global shipping routes.

item.title: US Justice Department indicts former FBI Director Comey
item.description: The Justice Department has indicted former FBI Director James Comey, marking an escalation in the Trump administration's conflicts with former law enforcement officials.

item.title: Fed sails into uncharted waters as Powell bows out
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is expected to step down as the central bank navigates unprecedented challenges from the Iran conflict and global economic uncertainty.
